---
title: "Extensions"
---

The previous part established the core linear-model framework. This part extends
that framework to the situations where the basic assumptions no longer describe
the data well enough: non-independence, grouped structure, non-Gaussian responses,
smooth nonlinear trends, mechanistic nonlinear forms, conditional quantiles, and
prediction-oriented regularisation.

These chapters should not be read as a collection of advanced tricks. Each method
responds to a particular kind of mismatch between question, design, data, and
model. The point is therefore not only to learn a new function or model family,
but to recognise the specific problem that makes the extension necessary.

By this stage, the statistical toolkit is broad. The challenge is judgement:
knowing when the simple model is still defensible, and when the structure of the
data requires something richer.
